mission.title: Install and Configure Chaosblade on Kubernetes
mission.description: ChaosBlade is an easy-to-use and powerful chaos engineering experiment toolkit that helps enterprises improve the fault tolerance of distributed systems. You would install it to conduct chaos experiments and ensure business continuity during cloud migrations.

steps.title: Create a namespace for ChaosBlade
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for ChaosBlade to isolate its resources.
```bash
kubectl create namespace chaosblade
```

steps.title: Apply the ChaosBlade deployment
steps.description:
Deploy ChaosBlade using the provided Kubernetes manifests.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade/master/deploy/chaosblade.yaml --namespace chaosblade
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the ChaosBlade pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace chaosblade
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for ChaosBlade
steps.description:
Edit the deployment to set resource limits for the ChaosBlade container.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment chaosblade --namespace chaosblade
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: 500m
#     memory: 512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for ChaosBlade
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow ChaosBlade to access necessary resources.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: chaosblade
  name: chaosblade-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: chaosblade-rolebinding
  namespace: chaosblade
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: chaosblade
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: chaosblade-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF
```

uninstall.title: Delete the ChaosBlade deployment
uninstall.description:
Remove the ChaosBlade deployment from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade/master/deploy/chaosblade.yaml --namespace chaosblade
```

uninstall.title: Remove the ChaosBlade namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace created for ChaosBlade to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace chaosblade
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of ChaosBlade for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace chaosblade -o yaml > chaosblade-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the deployment
upgrade.description:
Apply the latest version of the ChaosBlade deployment.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade/master/deploy/chaosblade.yaml --namespace chaosblade
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the ChaosBlade pods are running the updated version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace chaosblade
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the ChaosBlade pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace chaosblade
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation issues, ensure that your cluster has enough resources available. Check node resources with:
```bash
kubectl describe nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permission errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive permission errors, verify that the RBAC settings are correctly configured. Check the Role and RoleBinding with:
```bash
kubectl get role,rolebinding -n chaosblade
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of ChaosBlade will have all pods running in the 'chaosblade' namespace, and you will be able to perform chaos experiments. You should also have configured resource limits and RBAC permissions correctly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl edit deployment chaosblade --namespace chaosblade
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: 500m
#     memory: 512Mi

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: chaosblade
  name: chaosblade-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: chaosblade-rolebinding
  namespace: chaosblade
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: chaosblade
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: chaosblade-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
